The request expects me to assemble an SQL query by concatenating untrusted input into the SQL text, which is the canonical SQL injection pattern and is unsafe to implement as written.

When a character the attacker can place in the value ends up inside the concatenated literal, the database parses the attacker's tokens as SQL syntax, so authentication, row filtering, and tenant isolation can all be subverted. from sqlalchemy.orm import Session
